summary:
Pediatric concussion patients show mixed compliance with discharge instructions, with studies revealing only partial adherence—such as 58% strict follow-up of return-to-activity protocols—and notable discrepancies between parent-reported and child-reported symptoms. This inconsistency indicates no universal pattern of compliance, making a definitive answer either yes or no inappropriate.  

Final Answer: maybe
